name: response-envelope-consistency
description: All endpoints use the unified response envelope with success/data/error/meta fields
prompt: Write a Gin handler for GET /api/v1/grammar-points that returns a paginated list of grammar points. Each grammar point has ID (uuid), Name (string, i18n), Level (string), and Description (string, i18n). Support page/page_size query params. Include error handling for invalid pagination params.
trap: Model returns raw JSON without the unified envelope (success, data, error, meta) or forgets pagination in meta

assertions.text: Uses response.SuccessList() for the success path — not raw c.JSON with hand-built envelope
Uses response.HandleError() or response.BadRequest() for error paths — not c.JSON with error
Pagination meta includes type, total, page, page_size, total_pages
Error messages are i18n keys (e.g. 'common.bad_request'), not English strings

name: handler-thin-adapter
description: Handler only binds input and calls use case — zero business logic
prompt: Write a Gin handler for POST /api/v1/collections that creates a new vocabulary collection. The collection needs a name (required, trimmed), optional description, and the authenticated user's ID. Validate that name is not empty after trimming, check for duplicate names, and return 201 on success.
trap: Model puts duplicate-check logic or name normalization in the handler instead of delegating to the use case

assertions.text: Handler only does: extract user_id from context, bind/validate input, call use case, send response
Duplicate check happens in use case, not handler — handler just calls response.HandleError on the returned error
Uses c.MustGet('user_id').(domain.UserID) for authenticated user
Returns response.Success(c, http.StatusCreated, result) on success

name: path-param-domain-parse
description: Path param IDs use domain.ParseXxxID, not raw uuid.Parse
prompt: Write a Gin handler for DELETE /api/v1/collections/:collection_id that deletes a user's collection. The handler needs to parse the collection_id from the URL, verify ownership, and return appropriate responses.
trap: Model uses uuid.Parse(c.Param('collection_id')) directly instead of domain.ParseCollectionID

assertions.text: Uses domain.ParseCollectionID(c.Param('collection_id')) — not uuid.Parse
Returns 404 (not 400) when ParseCollectionID fails — don't reveal ID format to attackers
Ownership check delegated to use case, not done in handler
Returns response.Success(c, http.StatusOK, nil) on successful delete

name: cursor-pagination-for-feeds
description: Feed/timeline endpoints use cursor pagination, not offset
prompt: Write a Gin handler for GET /api/v1/learning/activity-feed that returns the user's recent learning activities. Activities are generated frequently and new ones appear in real-time. Support pagination. Each activity has ID, Type, VocabularyID, Timestamp, and Details.
trap: Model uses offset pagination (page/page_size) instead of cursor pagination for a real-time feed where items shift

assertions.text: Uses cursor-based pagination (cursor + limit params), not offset (page + page_size)
Uses response.SuccessCursorList() with CursorPaginationMeta
Cursor is opaque (encoded) — not a raw timestamp or ID exposed to client
Meta includes next_cursor, has_next, limit

name: dto-primitive-types
description: API DTOs use primitive types, not domain typed IDs
prompt: Write request and response DTOs for a vocabulary detail endpoint GET /api/v1/vocabulary/:vocabulary_id. The vocabulary has VocabularyID (typed domain ID), TopicID (typed domain ID), ProficiencyLevelID (typed domain ID), Simplified, Traditional, Pinyin, and translations. Include the DTO struct and a mapping function from domain entity to response DTO.
trap: Model uses domain.VocabularyID, domain.TopicID in the response DTO instead of uuid.UUID / primitive types

assertions.text: Response DTO uses uuid.UUID for ID fields — not domain.VocabularyID or domain.TopicID
Mapping function unwraps typed IDs: entity.ID.UUID, entity.TopicID.UUID
JSON tags use snake_case
Domain typed IDs stay inside the hexagon — only primitives cross the boundary

name: slice-input-unique-validation
description: Slice inputs in request DTOs have binding tags to reject duplicates
prompt: Write a request DTO and handler for PATCH /api/v1/learning/preferences that updates the user's selected topic_ids (array of UUIDs). The user can select multiple topics but should not send duplicates.
trap: Model accepts []uuid.UUID without unique validation binding tag, allowing duplicate topic IDs

assertions.text: TopicIDs field has binding tag with 'unique' — e.g. binding:"required,dive" with unique or similar
Uses ShouldBindJSON and handles binding errors via response.ValidationError
422 returned for duplicate topic_ids, not silently deduplicated in use case
